Science.detailedSummary: Researchers who watched Orbán dismantle academic freedom and strip universities of their autonomy, and saw Hungary blocked from receiving some funding from the European Union, are now cautiously hopeful that his successor, Péter Magyar, will repair the damage. Orbán’s systematic erosion of university independence became a model for the changes to the research system now under way in the United States. But how far those changes will reach in the university and research sectors remains to be seen, and researchers warn that simply restoring the old system might not be enough to repair the damage done to the Hungarian university sector.“What will happen to Hungarian science now is still very uncertain and unpredictable, but at least we have some hope that the future will bring some change,” says Imola Wilhelm, a neuroscientist at the Biological Research Centre in Szeged, Hungary. pages.detailedSummary: Researchers who watched Orbán dismantle academic freedom and strip universities of their autonomy, and saw Hungary blocked from receiving some funding from the European Union, are now cautiously hopeful that his successor, Péter Magyar, will repair the damage. Orbán’s systematic erosion of university independence became a model for the changes to the research system now under way in the United States. But how far those changes will reach in the university and research sectors remains to be seen, and researchers warn that simply restoring the old system might not be enough to repair the damage done to the Hungarian university sector.“What will happen to Hungarian science now is still very uncertain and unpredictable, but at least we have some hope that the future will bring some change,” says Imola Wilhelm, a neuroscientist at the Biological Research Centre in Szeged, Hungary.
